# Edge of Context

> Practical AI engineering by Slava Dubrov.

## Articles

- [Model Quantization in 2026: From Foundations to Production Serving](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/07/05/model-quantization-in-2026-from-foundations-to-production-serving/): A practical guide to choosing LLM and diffusion-model quantization paths by bottleneck, hardware, runtime kernel, calibration data, and deploy checks.
- [AI Agent Memory: Schema-Guided Typed State for Long-Running Systems](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/06/20/schema-guided-agent-memory/): Why production AI agents need schema-guided memory with temporal validity, provenance, and structured conflict handling instead of raw text vector recall.
- [Evaluating AI Agents in Production: From Traces to Test Suites](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/06/10/agent-evals-traces-to-test-suites/): How to evaluate production AI agents by turning traces into versioned regression datasets, trajectory metrics, calibrated judges, and CI gates.
- [Long-Running AI Agent Runtime in 2026: Sessions, Sandboxes, Checkpoints, and Harnesses](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/05/26/ai-agent-runtime/): How to run long-running AI agents in production with sessions, harnesses, sandboxes, checkpoints, traces, and deployment patterns that recover cleanly.
- [Evaluating RAG: Metrics for Every Stage of a Production RAG System](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/05/10/rag-evaluation-metrics/): RAG evaluation metrics for parsing, retrieval, reranking, generation, citations, and production telemetry, with code and failure modes.
- [AI Agent Security in 2026: Guardrails, Permissions, Sandboxes, and MCP Threats](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/04/20/ai-agent-security/): Why LLM guardrails are not enough for AI agent security, and where permissions, sandboxes, HITL, MCP scoping, and policy checks fit.
- [The Definitive Guide to NER in 2026: Encoders, LLMs, and the 3-Tier Production Architecture](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/04/02/ner-guide/): NER in 2026 means choosing between GLiNER, spaCy, Transformers, and LLM extraction for latency, accuracy, and schema control.
- [AI Agent Tool Use in 2026: MCP, CLI, Skills, and Code Execution](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/03/24/ai-agent-tool-use/): Compare JSON tool calling, MCP, Skills, CLI, and code execution for AI agents, with ACI design rules and the production trade-offs that matter.
- [LLM Engineering Guide: 45 Concepts for Inference, Training, Architecture, and Operations](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/03/13/llm-engineering-guide/): A practitioner's reference to 45 LLM engineering concepts for production systems, spanning inference, training, architecture, deployment, and operations.
- [The Definitive Guide to OCR in 2026: From Pipelines to VLMs](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/03/04/ocr-guide/): OCR in 2026 means choosing between classical pipelines and VLMs for text, layout, tables, and document extraction.
- [Modern Data Processing Engines Compared: Polars, DataFusion, Daft, Ray Data, Pandas, and Spark](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/02/21/modern-data-processing-engines/): Benchmarks comparing Polars, DataFusion, Daft, Ray Data, Pandas, and Spark on tabular and multimodal workloads, with code and decision rules.
- [AI Agent Memory Architecture in 2026: Checkpoints, Vector Stores, and File-Based Memory](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/02/14/ai-agent-memory-architecture/): How to design AI agent memory with checkpoints, PostgreSQL or Redis, vector stores such as Qdrant, and file-based memory for long-running systems.
- [Search Ranking Stack in 2026: BM25, Embeddings, Cross-Encoders, and LLM Reranking](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/02/08/search-ranking-stack/): How to build a search ranking stack with BM25, dense embeddings, hybrid RRF, cross-encoder reranking, and LLM listwise reranking on Amazon ESCI.
- [AI Agent Reasoning Loops in 2026: ReAct vs ReWOO vs Plan-and-Execute](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/01/31/ai-agent-reasoning-loops/): Compare ReAct, ReWOO, and Plan-and-Execute for AI agents. LangGraph examples show how each loop trades adaptability for cost, latency, and planning structure.
- [Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections (mHC): DeepSeek Residual Scaling Explained](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/01/21/manifold-constrained-hyper-connections/): A technical walkthrough of DeepSeek's Manifold-Constrained Hyper-Connections (mHC), residual stream width scaling, Sinkhorn routing, and training stability.
- [Enterprise RAG Challenge 3 (ECR3): Winning AI Agent Architectures](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/01/11/enterprise-rag-challenge-agent-architectures/): What public Enterprise RAG Challenge 3 submissions reveal about prompt iteration, validation, context strategy, tool wrappers, and agent architecture.
- [LLM Fine-Tuning Guide: LoRA, QLoRA, DoRA, Unsloth, Axolotl, and Deployment](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2026/01/04/llm-fine-tuning-guide/): When to fine-tune LLMs, when to use RAG or prompting, and how LoRA, QLoRA, DoRA, Unsloth, Axolotl, datasets, evals, and deployment fit.
- [Schema-Guided Reasoning on vLLM: Structured Outputs with xgrammar and Pydantic](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/12/28/schema-guided-reasoning-vllm/): How Schema-Guided Reasoning uses vLLM, xgrammar, Pydantic schemas, and constrained decoding to make LLM outputs structured and reliable.
- [LoRAX Serving Guide: Operating Many LoRA Adapters on Kubernetes](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/10/22/lorax-serving-guide-lora-adapters-kubernetes/): Evaluate and deploy LoRAX for long-tail LoRA serving, with current APIs, Kubernetes chart hardening, adapter security, cache-aware routing, and vLLM trade-offs.
- [Domain-Driven Design for AI Agents: Bounded Contexts, Tools, and Business Rules](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/10/20/domain-driven-design-ai-agents/): How domain-driven design helps AI agents use shared language, bounded contexts, application services, aggregates, repositories, and deterministic business rules.
- [Context Engineering for AI Agents: Context Windows, Memory, Tools, and Guardrails](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/10/05/context-engineering-ai-agents/): How to design context engineering for AI agents: context windows, instruction hierarchy, retrieval, memory, tools, guardrails, provenance, and compression.
- [MCP Server Tutorial with uv and FastMCP: Build a FeatureStoreLite Server](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/06/10/mcp-server-tutorial-uv-fastmcp/): Build a custom MCP server with uv and FastMCP, expose ML feature-store tools, test them locally, and connect the server to Claude Desktop.
- [Open-Weight LLM Variants, Quantization, and Formats: Instruct, MoE, GGUF, GPTQ, and AWQ](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/05/11/open-source-llm-formats/): How to read open-weight model names by separating training role, architecture, quantization method, storage format, runtime, and hardware fit.
- [Stable Diffusion on macOS: Draw Things, DiffusionBee, ComfyUI, A1111, and Fooocus](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/05/10/stable-diffusion-macos-tools/): Compare local image-generation tools on macOS by setup, model support, workflow control, reproducibility, extensions, and maintenance status.
- [Local LLMs on macOS: Ollama, LM Studio, llama.cpp, MLX, and Apple Silicon](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/05/10/local-llms-on-macos/): Compare local LLM tools on macOS by interface, model format, memory headroom, API exposure, and Apple Silicon development workflow.
- [`pyproject.toml` Guide: Python Packaging, Dependencies, and Tool Configuration](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/05/08/pyproject-toml-guide/): How pyproject.toml works for build systems, project metadata, runtime dependencies, development groups, CLI entry points, and tool configuration.
- [Zsh Startup Files: `~/.zprofile` vs `~/.zshrc` on macOS and Linux](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/05/07/zprofile-vs-zshrc/): Zsh startup files explained: when ~/.zprofile and ~/.zshrc load on macOS and Linux, what belongs in each file, and how to diagnose the running shell mode.
- [MLOps vs LLMOps: Infrastructure for Foundation Models and LLM Systems](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/05/06/mlops-vs-llmops/): What foundation models changed in MLOps: release units, retrieval, tools, evaluation, tracing, serving, and operational risk.
- [Scaling Large Language Models: Multi-GPU and Multi-Node Strategies That Hold Up in Practice](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/05/04/scaling-llms-multi-gpu-multi-node/): How to scale large language models across GPUs and nodes with data, fully sharded, tensor, pipeline, context, and expert parallelism.
- [MacBook Setup for AI Engineering: macOS Tools, Python, Docker, and Terminal Workflow](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/04/19/macbook-ai-engineering-setup/): A MacBook setup checklist for AI engineering: Xcode tools, Homebrew, Python with uv, Docker, terminal setup, and VS Code.
- [uv on macOS: Managing Python Versions, Projects, and Tools](https://slavadubrov.github.io/blog/2025/04/17/uv-python-on-macos/): How I use uv on macOS for Python installs, project dependencies, lock files, inline scripts, and one-off CLI tools.
